std::string.description: Dynamic character string class providing automatic memory management, concatenation, comparison, and search operations.

size.description: Returns the number of characters in the string.

length.description: Returns the number of characters (same as size).

empty.description: Returns true if the string has no characters.

data.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying character array.

substr.signature: string substr(size_type pos = 0, size_type count = npos) const
substr.description: Returns a substring starting at pos with length count.

find.signature: size_type find(const string& str, size_type pos = 0) const noexcept
find.description: Finds the first occurrence of the substring.

rfind.signature: size_type rfind(const string& str, size_type pos = npos) const noexcept
rfind.description: Finds the last occurrence of the substring.

append.description: Appends the given string to the end.

insert.signature: string& insert(size_type pos, const string& str)
insert.description: Inserts characters at the specified position.

erase.signature: string& erase(size_type pos = 0, size_type count = npos)

replace.signature: string& replace(size_type pos, size_type count, const string& str)
replace.description: Replaces part of the string with another string.

compare.signature: int compare(const string& str) const noexcept

starts_with.description: Checks if the string starts with the given prefix (C++20).

ends_with.description: Checks if the string ends with the given suffix (C++20).

contains.description: Checks if the string contains the given substring (C++23).

clear.description: Clears the contents of the string.

push_back.description: Appends a character to the end.

begin.description: Returns an iterator to the first character.

end.description: Returns an iterator past the last character.

std::vector.signature: template<class T, class Allocator = std::allocator<T>> class vector
std::vector.description: Dynamic contiguous array with automatic memory management. Supports amortized O(1) push_back and O(1) random access. The most commonly used container in C++.

push_back.description: Appends an element to the end of the vector.

emplace_back.description: Constructs an element in-place at the end.

empty.description: Returns true if the vector has no elements.

capacity.description: Returns the number of elements that can be held in currently allocated storage.

reserve.description: Reserves storage for at least new_cap elements.

resize.description: Resizes the container to contain count elements.

clear.description: Erases all elements from the vector.

at.description: Returns element at pos with bounds checking.

front.description: Returns a reference to the first element.

back.description: Returns a reference to the last element.

data.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying array.

insert.signature: iterator insert(const_iterator pos, const T& value)
insert.description: Inserts an element before the given position.

erase.description: Erases the element at the given position.

begin.description: Returns an iterator to the first element.

end.description: Returns an iterator past the last element.

rbegin.description: Returns a reverse iterator to the last element.

rend.description: Returns a reverse iterator before the first element.

std::map.signature: template<class Key, class T, class Compare = std::less<Key>, class Allocator = std::allocator<std::pair<const Key, T>>> class map
std::map.description: Sorted associative container storing unique key-value pairs, ordered by keys using a red-black tree. O(log n) lookup, insertion, and removal.

insert.description: Inserts a key-value pair if the key does not exist.

emplace.signature: template<class... Args> std::pair<iterator, bool> emplace(Args&&... args)

erase.description: Removes the element with the given key.

find.description: Finds an element with the given key.

count.description: Returns the number of elements with the given key (0 or 1).

contains.description: Checks if the map contains an element with the given key (C++20).

at.description: Returns a reference to the mapped value with bounds checking.

operator[].description: Returns a reference to the mapped value, inserting a default if not present.

size.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs.

empty.description: Returns true if the map is empty.

begin.description: Returns an iterator to the first element.

end.description: Returns an iterator past the last element.

lower_bound.description: Returns an iterator to the first element not less than key.

upper_bound.description: Returns an iterator to the first element greater than key.

std::unordered_map.signature: template<class Key, class T, class Hash = std::hash<Key>, class KeyEqual = std::equal_to<Key>, class Allocator = std::allocator<std::pair<const Key, T>>> class unordered_map
std::unordered_map.description: Hash-based associative container storing unique key-value pairs. Average O(1) lookup, insertion, and removal.

insert.description: Inserts a key-value pair if the key does not exist.

emplace.signature: template<class... Args> std::pair<iterator, bool> emplace(Args&&... args)

erase.description: Removes the element with the given key.

find.description: Finds an element with the given key.

count.description: Returns the number of elements with the given key (0 or 1).

contains.description: Checks if the map contains an element with the given key (C++20).

at.description: Returns a reference to the mapped value with bounds checking.

operator[].description: Returns a reference to the mapped value, inserting a default if not present.

empty.description: Returns true if the map is empty.

load_factor.description: Returns the average number of elements per bucket.

reserve.description: Reserves space for at least count elements.

std::set.signature: template<class Key, class Compare = std::less<Key>, class Allocator = std::allocator<Key>> class set
std::set.description: Sorted associative container of unique elements implemented as a red-black tree. O(log n) insertion, removal, and lookup.

insert.description: Inserts an element if not already present.

emplace.signature: template<class... Args> std::pair<iterator, bool> emplace(Args&&... args)

erase.description: Removes the element with the given value.

find.description: Finds an element with the given value.

count.description: Returns how many elements match the key (0 or 1).

contains.description: Checks if the set contains the given value (C++20).

empty.description: Returns true if the set is empty.

begin.description: Returns an iterator to the first element.

end.description: Returns an iterator past the last element.

lower_bound.description: Returns an iterator to the first element not less than key.

upper_bound.description: Returns an iterator to the first element greater than key.

std::unique_ptr.signature: template<class T, class Deleter = std::default_delete<T>> class unique_ptr
std::unique_ptr.description: Smart pointer with exclusive ownership semantics. Cannot be copied, only moved. Automatically deletes the managed object when the unique_ptr goes out of scope.

get.description: Returns the stored pointer without releasing ownership.

release.description: Releases ownership of the managed object and returns the raw pointer.

reset.description: Replaces the managed object, deleting the old one.

swap.description: Swaps the managed objects and associated deleters.

operator->.description: Returns the stored pointer for member access.

operator bool.description: Checks if the pointer is non-null.

std::shared_ptr.description: Smart pointer with shared (reference-counted) ownership. Multiple shared_ptr instances can own the same object. The object is destroyed when the last shared_ptr owning it is destroyed.

reset.description: Releases ownership. If last owner, deletes the object.

use_count.description: Returns the number of shared_ptr instances sharing ownership.

unique.description: Returns true if this is the only owner (deprecated in C++17, removed in C++20).

operator->.description: Returns the stored pointer for member access.

operator bool.description: Checks if the pointer is non-null.

std::optional.description: Manages an optional contained value — a value that may or may not be present. Useful as a return type for functions that may fail without using exceptions or sentinel values. (C++17)

value.description: Returns the contained value with bounds checking (throws std::bad_optional_access if empty).

value_or.signature: template<class U> T value_or(U&& default_value) const&
value_or.description: Returns the contained value if present, otherwise the given default.

has_value.description: Returns true if the optional contains a value.

reset.description: Destroys the contained value, making the optional empty.

operator*.description: Accesses the contained value (no bounds checking).

operator->.description: Accesses members of the contained value.

operator bool.description: Returns true if the optional contains a value.

std::array.signature: template<class T, std::size_t N> struct array
std::array.description: Fixed-size container wrapping a C-style array. Provides STL-compatible interface with bounds checking via at(). Stack-allocated with zero overhead compared to built-in arrays.

at.description: Returns element at pos with bounds checking.

operator[].description: Returns element at pos (no bounds checking).

data.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying C-style array.

size.description: Returns the number of elements (always N).

empty.description: Returns true if the array is empty (N == 0).

fill.description: Fills all elements with the given value.

begin.description: Returns an iterator to the first element.

end.description: Returns an iterator past the last element.

std::pair.signature: template<class T1, class T2> struct pair
std::pair.description: Holds two heterogeneous objects as a single unit. Commonly used as the value type of std::map and as a return type for multiple values.

first.description: The first element of the pair.

second.description: The second element of the pair.

std::tuple.description: Fixed-size heterogeneous collection of values. Generalizes std::pair to an arbitrary number of elements. Supports structured bindings (C++17).

std::get.signature: template<std::size_t I, class... Types> auto& get(tuple<Types...>& t) noexcept
std::get.description: Extracts the I-th element from the tuple.

std::tuple_size.description: Provides the number of elements in a tuple at compile time.

std::tuple_element.signature: template<std::size_t I, class T> struct tuple_element
std::tuple_element.description: Provides the type of the I-th element of a tuple.

std::make_tuple.description: Creates a tuple from the given arguments.

std::tie.signature: template<class... Types> tuple<Types&...> tie(Types&... args) noexcept
std::tie.description: Creates a tuple of lvalue references.

std::function.signature: template<class R, class... Args> class function<R(Args...)>
std::function.description: General-purpose polymorphic function wrapper. Can store, copy, and invoke any callable target — functions, lambdas, bind expressions, and other function objects.

operator().description: Invokes the stored callable with the given arguments.

operator bool.description: Returns true if the function object has a callable target.

target.description: Returns a pointer to the stored callable if type matches, else nullptr.

std::weak_ptr.description: Non-owning smart pointer that holds a weak reference to a std::shared_ptr-managed object. Does not affect the reference count. Used to break circular references.

lock.description: Creates a shared_ptr that shares ownership of the managed object. Returns empty shared_ptr if expired.

expired.description: Returns true if the managed object has been deleted.

use_count.description: Returns the number of shared_ptr instances sharing ownership.

reset.description: Releases the reference to the managed object.

std::make_unique.signature: template<class T, class... Args> unique_ptr<T> make_unique(Args&&... args)
std::make_unique.description: Constructs an object of type T and wraps it in a std::unique_ptr. Preferred over using new directly. Exception-safe. (C++14)

std::make_shared.signature: template<class T, class... Args> shared_ptr<T> make_shared(Args&&... args)
std::make_shared.description: Constructs an object of type T and wraps it in a std::shared_ptr. Performs a single allocation for both the object and the control block.

std::move.signature: template<class T> constexpr std::remove_reference_t<T>&& move(T&& t) noexcept
std::move.description: Casts its argument to an rvalue reference, indicating that it may be moved from. Enables move semantics for efficient transfer of resources.

std::forward.signature: template<class T> constexpr T&& forward(std::remove_reference_t<T>& t) noexcept
std::forward.description: Forwards lvalues as lvalues and rvalues as rvalues. Used in template code to implement perfect forwarding.

std::sort.signature: template<class RandomIt> void sort(RandomIt first, RandomIt last)
std::sort.description: Sorts elements in [first, last) in ascending order. Uses introsort (hybrid of quicksort, heapsort, and insertion sort). Average and worst-case O(n log n).

std::find.signature: template<class InputIt, class T> InputIt find(InputIt first, InputIt last, const T& value)
std::find.description: Returns an iterator to the first element in [first, last) that equals value. Returns last if not found. O(n).

std::accumulate.signature: template<class InputIt, class T> T accumulate(InputIt first, InputIt last, T init)
std::accumulate.description: Computes the sum of the given initial value and the elements in the range [first, last). Can also accept a custom binary operation.

std::transform.signature: template<class InputIt, class OutputIt, class UnaryOp> OutputIt transform(InputIt first, InputIt last, OutputIt d_first, UnaryOp op)
std::transform.description: Applies the given function to each element in [first, last) and stores the result in the range beginning at d_first.

std::for_each.signature: template<class InputIt, class UnaryFunc> UnaryFunc for_each(InputIt first, InputIt last, UnaryFunc f)
std::for_each.description: Applies the given function to each element in [first, last). Returns the function object after all applications.

std::swap.signature: template<class T> void swap(T& a, T& b) noexcept(...)
std::swap.description: Exchanges the values of a and b. Uses move semantics when available.

std::string_view.description: A lightweight non-owning reference to a string or substring. Provides a read-only view without copying. Zero overhead. (C++17)

empty.description: Returns true if the view is empty.

data.signature: constexpr const char* data() const noexcept
data.description: Returns a pointer to the underlying character array.

substr.signature: constexpr string_view substr(size_type pos = 0, size_type count = npos) const
substr.description: Returns a view of the substring.

find.signature: constexpr size_type find(string_view sv, size_type pos = 0) const noexcept
find.description: Finds the first occurrence of the given substring.

starts_with.signature: constexpr bool starts_with(string_view sv) const noexcept
starts_with.description: Checks if the view starts with the given prefix (C++20).

ends_with.signature: constexpr bool ends_with(string_view sv) const noexcept
ends_with.description: Checks if the view ends with the given suffix (C++20).

remove_prefix.description: Shrinks the view by moving the start forward by n characters.

remove_suffix.description: Shrinks the view by moving the end backward by n characters.

std::variant.description: Type-safe discriminated union. Holds a value of one of the specified alternative types at any given time. Never empty (unless valueless_by_exception). (C++17)

index.description: Returns the zero-based index of the currently held alternative.

valueless_by_exception.description: Returns true if the variant is in an invalid state due to an exception.

emplace.signature: template<class T, class... Args> T& emplace(Args&&... args)
emplace.description: Constructs the given alternative type in-place.

swap.description: Swaps the contents with another variant.

std::span.signature: template<class T, std::size_t Extent = std::dynamic_extent> class span
std::span.description: A lightweight non-owning reference to a contiguous sequence of objects. Replacement for (pointer, length) pairs. (C++20)

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the span.

data.description: Returns a pointer to the beginning of the sequence.

empty.description: Returns true if the span has no elements.

subspan.signature: template<std::size_t Offset, std::size_t Count = dynamic_extent> constexpr span<T, ...> subspan() const
subspan.description: Returns a subspan starting at Offset.

first.signature: template<std::size_t Count> constexpr span<T, Count> first() const

last.signature: template<std::size_t Count> constexpr span<T, Count> last() const
